what: Create sampled data from standardized ecosystem output
title: Create sampled data from standardized ecosystem output
text: Ecosystem operating model. Generates data sets ecosystem model scenarios stock assessment models.

title: Create sampled data from standardized ecosystem output
text: Install recent version FIMSdiags using following code: using {pak}

title: Create sampled data from standardized ecosystem output
text: pkgdown site temporarily built add--demo branch currently active development.

what: Create Structural Equation Model (SEM) Equations and Time Series Data — create_dsem_inputs
title: Create Structural Equation Model (SEM) Equations and Time Series Data — create_dsem_inputs
text: function prepares inputs Dynamic Structural Equation Model (DSEM). generates specific path equations (drivers targets) compiles corresponding time-series data based environmental links diet composition thresholds.

title: Create Structural Equation Model (SEM) Equations and Time Series Data — create_dsem_inputs
Create Structural Equation Model (SEM) Equations and Time Series Data — create_dsem_inputs
text: data tibble/list containing data_om, data_environment, data_diet_composition. focal_functional_groups Character vector specifying one focal groups interest (e.g., c("menhaden 0", "menhaden 1")). diet_composition_threshold Numeric. minimum proportion diet required include predator-prey link SEM. Default 0.1, represent 10% diet.

title: Create Structural Equation Model (SEM) Equations and Time Series Data — create_dsem_inputs
text: tibble four columns: data_time_series_all complete matrix available time series. data_time_series_sem subset time series used specifically SEM. sem_tibble detailed tibble drivers, targets, lags, link types. sem_lines character string formatted dsem package.

title: Create Structural Equation Model (SEM) Equations and Time Series Data — create_dsem_inputs
text: function constructs three types linkages: Environmental: Links environmental indices impacted functional groups. Bottom-: Links prey biomass focal functional group. Top-: Links predator biomass focal functional group.

title: Create Structural Equation Model (SEM) Equations and Time Series Data — create_dsem_inputs
code: # Create mock data for demonstration data_om <- tibble::tibble(   year = rep(1984, 2),   month = rep(1, 2),   functional_group = c("Phytoplankton", "Menhaden (0yr)"),   functional_group_snake_case = c("phytoplankton", "menhaden_0yr"),   type = "biomass",   value = c(100, 50) )  data_environment <- tibble::tibble(   index = "amo",   year = 1984,   month = 1,   value = 0.679,   unit = "NA",   lag_months = 1,   impacted_group = "Menhaden (0yr)",   impacted_group_snake_case = "menhaden_0yr" )  data_diet_composition <- tibble::tibble(   prey = "Phytoplankton",   predator = "Menhaden (0yr)",   proportion = 0.5,   prey_snake_case = "phytoplankton",   predator_snake_case = "menhaden_0yr" )  data <- list(   data_om = list(data_om), data_environment = list(data_environment),   data_diet_composition = list(data_diet_composition) )  # Generate DSEM inputs dsem_inputs <- create_dsem_inputs(data, "Menhaden (0yr)", 0.1)  # Extract the SEM equations for use with the dsem package cat(dsem_inputs[["sem_lines"]]) #> amo -> menhaden_0yr, 1, amo_menhaden_0yr #> phytoplankton -> menhaden_0yr, 0, phytoplankton_menhaden_0yr  # Extract the time series data required for the SEM print(dsem_inputs[["data_time_series_sem"]][[1]]) #> Time Series: #> Start = 1  #> End = 1  #> Frequency = 1  #>   year month   amo phytoplankton menhaden_0yr #> 1 1984     1 0.679           100           50

what: Create SEM Equations from a Tibble — create_sem_equations
title: Create SEM Equations from a Tibble — create_sem_equations
text: helper function generates character string SEM equations formatted dsem package tibble SEM specifications.

title: Create SEM Equations from a Tibble — create_sem_equations
Create SEM Equations from a Tibble — create_sem_equations
text: sem_tibble tibble columns driver, target, lag, param_name.

title: Create SEM Equations from a Tibble — create_sem_equations
text: character string SEM equation new line.

title: Create SEM Equations from a Tibble — create_sem_equations
code: sem_tibble <- tibble::tibble(   driver = "sst", target = "menhaden_0yr", lag = 12, param_name = "sst_menhaden_0yr" ) create_sem_equations(sem_tibble) #> [1] "sst -> menhaden_0yr, 12, sst_menhaden_0yr"

what: Estimate true maturity parameters from ecosystem model input — estimate_true_maturity
title: Estimate true maturity parameters from ecosystem model input — estimate_true_maturity
text: Estimate true maturity parameters ecosystem model input

title: Estimate true maturity parameters from ecosystem model input — estimate_true_maturity
Estimate true maturity parameters from ecosystem model input — estimate_true_maturity
text: ages vector ages. spawning_proportion numeric vector spawning proportions (0 1) functional group within species. values can obtained Ecopath -> Input -> Basic input -> Edit Multi_Stanza Groups. functional_form character string specifying curve type. Currently supports "logistic".

title: Estimate true maturity parameters from ecosystem model input — estimate_true_maturity
text: named list containing estimated 'inflection_point' 'slope'.

what: Estimate true selectivity parameters from ecosystem model output — estimate_true_selectivity
title: Estimate true selectivity parameters from ecosystem model output — estimate_true_selectivity
text: function estimates selectivity curve parameters ecosystem model fishing mortality--age data. fits curves using Gamma distribution likelihood un-normalized values account mean-variance relationship.

title: Estimate true selectivity parameters from ecosystem model output — estimate_true_selectivity
code: estimate_true_selectivity(   data,   ages = NULL,   functional_form = c("logistic", "double_logistic") )

title: Estimate true selectivity parameters from ecosystem model output — estimate_true_selectivity
text: data tibble containing filtered ecosystem model output single species, typically generated calling load_model() followed get_truth(). Must contain columns: "species_name", "truth_label", "truth_type", "truth_time_step", "truth_group", "truth_year", "truth_unit", "truth_value". data pre-filtered single species; multiple species detected data, error issued. ages named numeric vector names correspond exactly unique labels truth_group column data, values represent actual structural representative midpoint ages. example: ages = c("0" = 0, "1" = 1, "2+" = 2) ages = c("juvenile" = 1, "adult" = 4). NULL (default), function attempt convert truth_group directly numeric values. fails, fall back sequential indexing issue warning. functional_form character string specifying selectivity curve type. Currently supports "logistic" "double_logistic".

title: Estimate true selectivity parameters from ecosystem model output — estimate_true_selectivity
text: tibble NOAA-FIMS/FIMS format estimated parameter values.

what: Ecopath with Ecosim (EwE) models for the Northwest Atlantic — ewe_ecosim_base_nwatlantic
title: Ecopath with Ecosim (EwE) models for the Northwest Atlantic — ewe_ecosim_base_nwatlantic
text: datasets contain tidy format tibbles two EwE model outputs Northwest Atlantic Ocean. ewe_ecosim_base_nwatlantic base model run, ewe_ecosim_with_environmental_data_nwatlantic run includes environmental data drivers. models loaded raw EwE files tidied using load_model function.

title: Ecopath with Ecosim (EwE) models for the Northwest Atlantic — ewe_ecosim_base_nwatlantic
Ecopath with Ecosim (EwE) models for the Northwest Atlantic — ewe_ecosim_base_nwatlantic
text: datasets tibbles 52272 rows 10 columns: file_name path file name, note path relative path. column helpful understanding EWE model run data came . type data type represented row. Valid types include: "catch" "biomass" "weight" "total_mortality" "fishing_mortality" (calculated catch divided biomass) "natural_mortality" (calculated total mortality minus fishing mortality) year four-digit integer specifying year. month one- two-digit integer specifying month. functional_group string specifying functional group row pertains . value real number containing value interest. species species name. group age stanza species (e.g., 0, 2-5, 6+). functional_group_snake_case functional group name snake_case. unit unit value. object class tbl_df (inherits tbl, data.frame) 52272 rows 10 columns.

title: Ecopath with Ecosim (EwE) models for the Northwest Atlantic — ewe_ecosim_base_nwatlantic
text: data prepared raw model files using scripts data-raw directory.

title: Ecopath with Ecosim (EwE) models for the Northwest Atlantic — ewe_ecosim_base_nwatlantic
text: Chagaris, D., Drew, K., Schueller, ., Cieri, M., Brito, J., Buchheister, . 2020. Ecological reference points Atlantic menhaden established using ecosystem model intermediate complexity. Frontiers Marine Science, 7:606417. 10.3389/fmars.2020.606417.

what: Get the functional groups from an EwE file — get_functional_groups
title: Get the functional groups from an EwE file — get_functional_groups
text: Functional group names useful column names output data EwE model, thus, function way get automatically EwE output. functional group names come basic estimates file.

title: Get the functional groups from an EwE file — get_functional_groups
Get the functional groups from an EwE file — get_functional_groups
text: file_path path EwE file.

title: Get the functional groups from an EwE file — get_functional_groups
text: tibble following columns: functional_group: exact functional group name used EwE. values match names order expected EwE output files load later. Keep column exact original EwE text possible audit trail back model. species: biological conceptual species/group name removing age, stage, size, within-species suffix. example, EwE "King mackerel (0-1yr)" "King mackerel (1+yr)", rows usually "King mackerel" species. group: age, stage, size, suffix within species. Use character value "0-1yr", "1+yr", "juvenile", "adult" functional group one several groups species. Use NA_character_ functional group within-species suffix. used delineate group within species. species multiple groups. functional_group_snake_case: unique, syntactically convenient name can used column name downstream output. names lowercase, contain spaces parentheses, unique across rows.

title: Get the functional groups from an EwE file — get_functional_groups
text: function convenience , required package. Using generate functional group names basic_estimates.csv file lessens burden realize function work every ecosystem model. work many common naming conventions, including species names followed numeric age groups, plus groups, ranges, small number text suffixes "juv" "adult". However, functional group names real EwE models always consistent. model uses names parsed correctly, can create functional group tibble pass tibble functions load_csv_ewe(). custom functional group tibble must contain one row functional group model four columns returned function. See return section details columns. create last column, functional_group_snake_case, consider calling split_functional_groups() original names manually edit species group needed. custom tibble checked used model-loading functions. particular, confirm functional_group missing values, every functional group name unique, functional_group_snake_case unique, number rows equals number living non-living groups EwE output files load. columns misaligned, downstream data can read wrong functional group. doubt, start output get_functional_groups() split_functional_groups(), inspect result, replace rows parsed incorrectly.

title: Get the functional groups from an EwE file — get_functional_groups
code: get_functional_groups(   file_path = fs::path(     system.file("extdata", package = "ecosystemom"),     "ewe_ecosim_with_environmental_data_nwatlantic", "basic_estimates.csv"   ) ) #> # A tibble: 22 × 4 #>    functional_group species      group functional_group_snake_case #>    <chr>            <chr>        <chr> <chr>                       #>  1 striped bass 0   striped bass 0     striped_bass_0              #>  2 striped bass 2-5 striped bass 2-5   striped_bass_2_5            #>  3 striped bass 6+  striped bass 6+    striped_bass_6_plus         #>  4 menhaden 0       menhaden     0     menhaden_0                  #>  5 menhaden 1       menhaden     1     menhaden_1                  #>  6 menhaden 2       menhaden     2     menhaden_2                  #>  7 menhaden 3       menhaden     3     menhaden_3                  #>  8 menhaden 4       menhaden     4     menhaden_4                  #>  9 menhaden 5       menhaden     5     menhaden_5                  #> 10 menhaden 6+      menhaden     6+    menhaden_6_plus             #> # ℹ 12 more rows  # If the automatic parser does not work for your naming convention, create # the functional group tibble yourself. This object can be supplied anywhere # ecosystemom asks for `functional_groups`. functional_groups <- tibble::tibble(   functional_group = c(     "King mackerel_(0-1yr)",     "King mackerel_(1+yr)",     "Detritus"   ),   species = c("King mackerel", "King mackerel", "Detritus"),   group = c("0-1yr", "1+yr", NA_character_),   functional_group_snake_case = c(     "king_mackerel_0_1yr",     "king_mackerel_1_plusyr",     "detritus"   ) )

title: Calculate
text: truth ecosystem model often different units needed sample . function takes output data load_model() calculates additional true information converting monthly age-composition information annual data. sampling performed just mathematical operations.

title: Calculate
Calculate
text: data tibble containing ecosystem model (e.g., Ecopath Ecosim) outputs load_model(). species_name string specifying desired species want summarize information . string must match entry data[["species"]] currently vector, just single string. See examples section code efficiently get output multiple species.

title: Calculate
text: nested tibble row holds output type (e.g., biomass, catch, numbers, weight) given time step category (e.g., monthly yearly). Thus, data within row returned output can expanded reveal full data, e.g., months output type. tibble following columns: species_name character string name species. truth_label character string indicating type data, e.g., "biomass", "catch", "numbers", "weight". truth_type character string specifying data index ("index") age-structured ("agecomp"). truth_time_step character string indicating temporal resolution data, either "monthly" "yearly". truth_om list-column containing nested data frames actual "truth" values. data frame can unnested includes: year year observation. month month observation. unit unit observation. value numeric value observation. group name group. groups can contain age ranges, plus groups, sub categories, e.g., juvenile. NA truth_type "index" instead "agecomp".

title: Calculate
text: following values calculated within function: biomass catch numbers--age dividing biomass--age weight--age information weight

title: Calculate
code: data(ewe_ecosim_base_nwatlantic) data <- ewe_ecosim_base_nwatlantic truth <- get_truth(   data,   species_name = "menhaden" ) #> ℹ The unit for numbers is currently set to "NA". Numbers need #> to be rescaled and filled in using `dplyr::mutate()` based on the underlying #> units of `biomass-at-age` and `weight-at-age`.  # To unnest tibble and view the data truth_long <- tidyr::unnest(truth, cols = c(truth_om))  # To calculate truth for a vector of species using {purrr} species_names <- c("menhaden", "striped bass", "bluefish") truth <- purrr::map_dfr(   species_names,   function(x) get_truth(data, species_name = x) ) #> ℹ The unit for numbers is currently set to "NA". Numbers need #> to be rescaled and filled in using `dplyr::mutate()` based on the underlying #> units of `biomass-at-age` and `weight-at-age`. #> ℹ The unit for numbers is currently set to "NA". Numbers need #> to be rescaled and filled in using `dplyr::mutate()` based on the underlying #> units of `biomass-at-age` and `weight-at-age`. #> ℹ The unit for numbers is currently set to "NA". Numbers need #> to be rescaled and filled in using `dplyr::mutate()` based on the underlying #> units of `biomass-at-age` and `weight-at-age`.

title: Load environmental data from a CSV file — load_csv_environmental_data
Load environmental data from a CSV file — load_csv_environmental_data
text: file_path string. Path CSV file containing environmental data. CSV file must contain following columns: index: Name environmental index (character). year: Year corresponding value (integer). month: Month corresponding value (integer). value: Value index (numeric). unit: Unit index (character). lag_months integer. lag environmental index functional group, months. example, lag_months = 12, environmental effect assumed influence functional group 12-month delay. impacted_group string indicating functional group impacted environmental index.

title: Load environmental data from a CSV file — load_csv_environmental_data
text: tibble containing environmental data lag functional group information.

title: Load diet composition data — load_diet_composition
text: function loads diet composition data CSV file standardizes tidy format. automatically detects format input file based column names processes accordingly.

title: Load diet composition data — load_diet_composition
Load diet composition data — load_diet_composition
text: file_path path CSV file containing diet composition data. verbose Logical. TRUE (default), prints informative messages.

title: Load diet composition data — load_diet_composition
text: tibble long format following columns: year: year observation (NA static Ecopath data). prey: name prey functional group. predator: name predator functional group. proportion: proportion prey predator's diet. prey_snake_case: prey name converted snake_case. predator_snake_case: predator name converted snake_case.

title: Load diet composition data — load_diet_composition
text: function currently supports three formats Ecopath Ecosim (EwE): Ecospace Yearly: Identified "Year" column. Data represents biomass consumed, converted proportions. Ecospace Monthly: Identified "TimeStep" column. format recognized yet supported. Ecopath: Identified "Prey \ predator" column. static diet matrix. function also handles empty column names can sometimes appear EwE outputs ignoring .

Load an ecosystem model — load_model
text: ... Arguments passed onto lower level load_model_()* functions. needed load_model_ewe_ecosim(), directory functional_groups. type string indicating type model data want load. default ewe_ecosim. Strings lower case text. verbose Logical. TRUE (default), prints informative messages.

title: Load an ecosystem model — load_model
text: tibble returned matches structure ewe_ecosim_base_nwatlantic.

what: Sample using a Dirichlet-multinomial distribution — sample_dirichlet_multinomial
title: Sample using a Dirichlet-multinomial distribution — sample_dirichlet_multinomial
text: Sample Dirichlet-multinomial distribution believe overdispersion present resulting data relative sampling multinomial distribution. , Dirichlet-multinomial distribution typically used compositional data fish certain age groups thought aggregate way uniform across age bins.

title: Sample using a Dirichlet-multinomial distribution — sample_dirichlet_multinomial
Sample using a Dirichlet-multinomial distribution — sample_dirichlet_multinomial
text: x numeric vector positive concentration parameters Dirichlet distribution sample . concentration parameters can thought pseudocounts. Smaller sums vector lead overdispersion resulting samples, .e., sum reaches infinity samples thought arrived multinomial distribution rather Dirichlet-multinomial. sample_size single, positive integer specifying total number trials (n) multinomial draw. example, sample_size = 20 assumed want 20 fish returned sample partitioned bins. default.

title: Sample using a Dirichlet-multinomial distribution — sample_dirichlet_multinomial
text: numeric vector one sample Dirichlet-multinomial distribution one entry per category. sum counts row equal input sample_size.

title: Sample using a Dirichlet-multinomial distribution — sample_dirichlet_multinomial
code: # Basic usage sample_dirichlet_multinomial(x = c(1, 2, 3), sample_size = 10) #> [1] 4 5 1  # More dispersed data # (smaller x values also known as alpha or concentration parameters) # Note: The total sum of alpha values influences the dispersion. # Smaller sum -> more dispersion. # Larger sum -> less dispersion (closer to multinomial). sample_dirichlet_multinomial(   x = c(0.1, 0.1, 0.1), sample_size = 20 ) #> [1] 11  9  0

title: Sample using a lognormal distribution — sample_lognormal
text: lognormal distribution used sample using mean natural log expected value minus input variance divided two input standard deviation. input variance calculated natural log coefficient variation squared plus one.

title: Sample using a lognormal distribution — sample_lognormal
code: # Note that setting the seed on a vectorized implementation of this function # will not lead to the same sample being returned for each element of x # because a single random number is generated for the entire vector. set.seed(123) sample_lognormal(1:10, sd = 1) #>  [1]  0.3462911  0.9636469  8.6479353  2.6033596  3.4512126 20.2230786 #>  [7]  6.7316909  1.3694104  2.7466218  3.8842236 set.seed(123) sample_lognormal(1, sd = 1) #> [1] 0.3462911 sample_lognormal(2, sd = 1) #> [1] 0.9636469  # You can also use the function with a vector of standard deviations set.seed(123) sample_lognormal(1:10, sd = 1:10) #>  [1] 3.462911e-01 1.708093e-01 3.577761e+00 1.779057e-03 3.556589e-05 #>  [6] 2.690978e-03 4.037354e-09 4.077497e-18 4.793452e-20 2.237642e-23

Sample using a multinomial distribution — sample_multinomial
text: x numeric vector counts per bin. values need integer values sum one converted proportions using proportions() prior passed prop argument rmultinom(). sample_size single, positive integer specifying number samples want partition bins. example, sample_size = 20 assumed want 20 fish returned sample partitioned bins. default.

title: Sample using a multinomial distribution — sample_multinomial
text: vector length x returned, sum vector equals input sample_size.

title: Sample using a multinomial distribution — sample_multinomial
text: Sampling multinomial distribution often used composition data simulated. example, operating model know many fish per age bin. number fish per age bin transformed proportions per age bin user can specify final sample size fish want returned vector. number fish per age bin often include fractional fish simulation numbers fish calculated biomass mean weight age. Thus, input vector x can integers, 0 1, real numbers. Everything work result always integers representing number fish per bin.

title: Sample using a multinomial distribution — sample_multinomial
code: # Sample from a multinomial distribution with 20 fish and 3 age bins set.seed(123) sample_multinomial(c(10, 5, 5), sample_size = 20) #> [1] 9 7 4

title: Split the functional groups into species and group — split_functional_groups
text: tibble following three columns: functional_group, species, group, functional_group_snake_case returned.

title: Split the functional groups into species and group — split_functional_groups
text: Kelli F. Johnson

title: Split the functional groups into species and group — split_functional_groups
code: # A hypothetical example of functional groups split_functional_groups(   c("spiny dogfish 0", "spiny dogfish 1-2", "spiny dogfish +") ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   functional_group  species       group functional_group_snake_case #>   <chr>             <chr>         <chr> <chr>                       #> 1 spiny dogfish 0   spiny dogfish 0     spiny_dogfish_0             #> 2 spiny dogfish 1-2 spiny dogfish 1-2   spiny_dogfish_1_2           #> 3 spiny dogfish +   spiny dogfish +     spiny_dogfish_plus          split_functional_groups(c("spiny dogfish", "spiny dogfish juvenile")) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   functional_group       species       group    functional_group_snake_case #>   <chr>                  <chr>         <chr>    <chr>                       #> 1 spiny dogfish          spiny dogfish NA       spiny_dogfish               #> 2 spiny dogfish juvenile spiny dogfish juvenile spiny_dogfish_juvenile      data("ewe_ecosim_base_nwatlantic", package = "ecosystemom") split_functional_groups(unique(ewe_ecosim_base_nwatlantic[["functional_group"]])) #> # A tibble: 22 × 4 #>    functional_group species      group functional_group_snake_case #>    <chr>            <chr>        <chr> <chr>                       #>  1 striped bass 0   striped bass 0     striped_bass_0              #>  2 striped bass 2-5 striped bass 2-5   striped_bass_2_5            #>  3 striped bass 6+  striped bass 6+    striped_bass_6_plus         #>  4 menhaden 0       menhaden     0     menhaden_0                  #>  5 menhaden 1       menhaden     1     menhaden_1                  #>  6 menhaden 2       menhaden     2     menhaden_2                  #>  7 menhaden 3       menhaden     3     menhaden_3                  #>  8 menhaden 4       menhaden     4     menhaden_4                  #>  9 menhaden 5       menhaden     5     menhaden_5                  #> 10 menhaden 6+      menhaden     6+    menhaden_6_plus             #> # ℹ 12 more rows

title: Create tests/testthat/test-*.R test file — use_testthat_template
text: helper function creates new test file testthat using template available within templates folder package. test file created "tests/testthat" directory name based input argument name. test file already exists, error message returned changes made.

title: Create tests/testthat/test-*.R test file — use_testthat_template
Create tests/testthat/test-*.R test file — use_testthat_template
text: name string providing name R function want test. name used create file name, .e., tests/testthat/test-{name}.R. name specified, function able create file name error returned.

title: Create tests/testthat/test-*.R test file — use_testthat_template
text: successful, function invisibly returns TRUE allow chaining commands. function unsuccessful, error message returned. Two messages also returned usethis package, used function. first states ecosystemom project computer. second states file path newly created file. file automatically opened.

title: Create tests/testthat/test-*.R test file — use_testthat_template
text: three minimum testing criteria ecosystemom, validated every R function within package. template file sets section three following test criteria: Input output correctness (IO correctness): ensure function behaves expected correct inputs returns expected outputs. Edge-case handling (Edge handling): validate function's performance invalid inputs unusual scenarios. Built-errors warnings (Error handling): confirm appropriate error warning messages triggered exceptional conditions. every expectation within test file call #' @description fits one line describing test. information used bookdown report testing results.

title: Create tests/testthat/test-*.R test file — use_testthat_template
code: # Create a new test file named "test-new_function" for `new_function()` if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ecosystemom:::use_testthat_template("new_function") } # }
